rationale: Weaver and Frederick (2012) is cited in the theory section as a paper that proposes bad-deal aversion as a driver of the endowment effect. The citing paper uses that mechanism to derive its own core predictions about when framing should affect WTP but not WTA, and then tests those predictions experimentally. It is also cited again in discussing prior endowment-effect evidence on buyer-seller differences, indicating an active conceptual role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Weaver and Frederick's bad-deal aversion/reference-price view of buyer-seller valuation and extends it to risky-prospect framing. It applies that mechanism to explain differential effects on WTP versus WTA and related uncertainty-effect patterns.
